required_rubric_dimensions: implementation feasibility against current module boundaries
host capability and coverage inference soundness
scope sequencing and 9.5–10 day cost credibility

second_model_availability.basis: Codex collaboration runtime exposed gpt-5.6-terra as an explicit model override distinct from the author lane

findings.claim: The proposed Contract 6 state has no authoritative, replayable basis for deciding a task's mutation-history coverage across host surfaces, so it can still infer full from a locally exact receipt despite an unobservable App/specialized interval.
findings.evidence: The initial candidate specified only per-fixture exhaustive surface and per-event host_profile, while the task projection had neither a task/episode capability binding nor an unobserved-interval marker; an invisible specialized patch emits no Pre/Post and runtime-only classification is lost on event replay.
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: The initial projection had no event-sourced capability lease or coverage interval, and current application authority is reconstructed entirely from the event ledger.

findings.claim: The Contract 5 cutover cannot safely guarantee the promised active-task escape path after subsequent installs, because retaining only the immediately previous runtime permits the legacy runtime to be pruned while active Contract 5 tasks still exist in other repositories.
findings.evidence: The initial candidate promised only previous-runtime retention while the current installer prunes every non-active runtime and has no global inventory of active tasks in arbitrary repositories.
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: install.mjs pruneRuntimes removes every runtime except the active hash, so a second Contract 6 install could delete the only Contract 5 escape binary.

findings.claim: The reconciliation acceptance suite does not prove compare-and-commit correctness when the repository candidate remains content-valid but its from_checkpoint is no longer the authoritative current checkpoint.
findings.evidence: The initial race coverage tested membership/stat/content changes but not a stale authority/checkpoint chain whose repository bytes happen to match the stale candidate.
findings.missing_check: Prepare two candidates from one checkpoint; commit one, return repository bytes to the other candidate, and prove the second cannot append a stale delta or authorize closure.
findings.gap_scope_reason: Without this check an append-only delta ledger could replay a transition that was never valid in the authority chain.
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: Content equality is insufficient because commitTaskCommand derives authority from the current event cursor; the plan lacked an explicit ABA checkpoint-chain oracle.

findings.claim: Capability leases still permit an exhaustive lease to cover a reconciled delta whose origin is an unobserved surface switch, so mutation-history coverage can incorrectly remain full.
findings.evidence: The revised candidate closed each artifact interval using the then-current long-lived lease without requiring a pre-operation reconciliation and operation-scoped causal boundary.
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: A long-lived exhaustive lease can misattribute an invisible delta to the prior surface; full coverage needs a pre-reconciled operation-scoped interval.

findings.claim: The plan needs an explicit per-event payload-version and reducer-dispatch matrix for Contract 5 replay versus Contract 6 writes.
findings.evidence: The revised plan explicitly versioned only task_opened while write_authorized and nested observation/review shapes also changed; current validators dispatch globally by event kind and payload v1.
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: The existing prims/event-store/task-engine validators key by kind and assume payload version 1, so reused changed events require an explicit two-version matrix.

findings.claim: The migration tests do not explicitly verify the promised cross-repository active-Contract-5 read-only and escape behavior after repeated Contract 6 installs.
findings.evidence: The plan promised read-only access and a pinned Runtime 5 escape path, while E15 and E18 separately tested migration and retention without exercising the complete external-repository path.
findings.missing_check: Create an active Contract 5 task outside the install source; perform initial activation and two Contract 6 upgrades; prove read-only status/audit/report, refusal of Contract 6 mutation, and real finish/abandon through the pinned Runtime 5.
findings.gap_scope_reason: Retention alone does not prove the binary remains a working escape path for another repository.
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: The previous E15/E18 split did not execute the promised cross-repository read-only and finish/abandon behavior end to end.

findings.claim: not-needed can bypass the newly mandatory complete-history requirement, allowing a critical or finite-write task to terminalize despite unknown/partial mutation history.
findings.evidence: The candidate made finite writes and critical tasks complete-history but its separate not-needed conditions omitted history, evidence/cursor freshness, and budget compliance; the current engine has a distinct direct not-needed terminal path.
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: The plan's not-needed branch lacked the common terminal assurance checks and current task-engine has an independent not-needed decider path.
